attack_surface.data_description: Wallet connection request and subsequent signing prompts presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User trusts the frontend's authenticity, allowing a spoofed site to request sensitive wallet operations or information.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A compromised frontend can trick the user into revealing sensitive information or signing malicious transactions upon wallet connection.

assumptions: User visits a malicious URL impersonating the Berachain Hub.
Wallet grants excessive permissions or signs transactions based on compromised frontend requests.
User is prompted for sensitive information like seed phrase by the malicious frontend.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users on phishing risks and wallet security best practices; wallet should clearly display requested permissions.

description: An attacker spoofs the Berachain Hub frontend to trick the user into connecting their wallet to a malicious site, potentially leading to seed phrase compromise or signing unintended transactions.

trace_source.doc_reference: How To Connect A Wallet With Berachain 🔌

attack_surface.data_description: Boost transaction parameters (validator ID, BGT amount) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User relies on frontend to accurately present transaction details before signing; wallet display may not be sufficient for verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter boost transaction details leading to loss of control over BGT.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters the validator ID or BGT amount in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear, verifiable details of the transaction being signed; users should be vigilant reviewing details.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for boosting a validator, causing the user to boost the wrong validator or an incorrect amount of BGT.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim incentives transaction parameters (recipient address, amount) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend dictates the recipient address for the claim transaction; user may not verify the address in the wallet prompt.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised frontend can redirect claimed incentive tokens to an attacker's wallet.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend sets the recipient address in the claim transaction to the attacker's address.
User signs the transaction without verifying the recipient address.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should clearly display the recipient address for asset transfers; users should always verify the recipient address.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend spoofs the claim process, causing the user to claim their earned incentive tokens to an attacker's wallet instead of their own.

attack_surface.data_description: Add liquidity transaction parameters (pool address, token amounts) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User relies on frontend to select the correct pool and amounts; wallet display may not be sufficient for verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter liquidity provision details leading to potential asset loss in a malicious pool.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters the pool address or token amounts in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear details of the contract interaction and parameters; users should verify the contract address.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for providing BEX liquidity, causing the user to deposit assets into a malicious or incorrect liquidity pool.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim BGT rewards transaction parameters (recipient address, amount) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: Frontend dictates the recipient address for the claim transaction; user may not verify the address in the wallet prompt.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised frontend can redirect claimed BGT rewards to an attacker's wallet.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend sets the recipient address in the claim transaction to the attacker's address.
User signs the transaction without verifying the recipient address.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should clearly display the recipient address for asset transfers; users should always verify the recipient address.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend spoofs the BGT claim process, causing the user to claim their earned BGT rewards to an attacker's wallet instead of their own.

attack_surface.data_description: Stake assets transaction parameters (vault address, token amounts) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User relies on frontend to select the correct vault and amounts; wallet display may not be sufficient for verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter staking details leading to potential asset loss in a malicious vault.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters the reward vault address or token amounts in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear details of the contract interaction and parameters; users should verify the contract address.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for staking assets in a reward vault, causing the user to stake into a malicious or incorrect vault.

attack_surface.data_description: Displayed list data and associated metadata for reward vaults.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User trusts the frontend to accurately display public on-chain data; no independent verification of list contents.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can display fake vault details leading to user interacting with malicious entities in subsequent steps.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend intercepts or modifies the display of public on-chain data.
User makes decisions based on the false information.

mitigation.strategy: Provide alternative, trusted sources for reviewing vaults; wallet could potentially verify interacted contract addresses against trusted lists.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend displays false information about active reward vaults (e.g., fake addresses, incorrect APRs) to trick the user into interacting with malicious entities.

attack_surface.data_description: Displayed list data and associated metadata for validators.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User trusts the frontend to accurately display public on-chain data; no independent verification of list contents.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can display fake validator details leading to user interacting with malicious entities in subsequent steps (e.g., boosting).

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend intercepts or modifies the display of public on-chain data.
User makes decisions based on the false information.

mitigation.strategy: Provide alternative, trusted sources for reviewing validators; wallet could potentially verify interacted contract addresses against trusted lists.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend displays false information about active validators (e.g., fake addresses, incorrect commission rates) to trick the user into interacting with malicious entities.

attack_surface.data_description: Redeem BGT transaction parameters (amount, recipient address for BERA) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User relies on frontend to accurately present redemption details; wallet display may not be sufficient for verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter redeem transaction details leading to loss of BGT or BERA.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters the BGT amount or the recipient address for BERA in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear details of the contract interaction and parameters; users should verify the recipient address.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for redeeming BGT for BERA, causing the user to redeem an incorrect amount or send the resulting BERA to an attacker's address.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap transaction parameters (token addresses, amounts, slippage, pool) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User relies on frontend to accurately present swap details; wallet display may not be sufficient for complex parameter verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter swap parameters (tokens, amounts, slippage) leading to unexpected token swaps or significant loss through slippage.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters swap parameters in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear details of the contract interaction and parameters; users should verify swap details and expected outcomes.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for swapping assets, causing the user to swap incorrect tokens, amounts, or accept excessive slippage.

attack_surface.data_description: Governance transaction parameters (proposal ID, vote choice, proposal details) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User relies on frontend to accurately present governance action details; wallet display may be insufficient for verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter governance action details, causing the user to vote against their will or propose harmful changes.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters the proposal ID, vote choice, or proposal content in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear details of the contract interaction and parameters; users should verify governance action details.

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for participating in governance, causing the user to vote on the wrong proposal, cast the wrong vote, or submit a malicious proposal.

attack_surface.data_description: Add incentive transaction parameters (vault address, token address, amount, rate) presented by frontend.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External User to Berachain Hub Frontend

security_gap.gap_description: User (Token Manager) relies on frontend to accurately present incentive details; wallet display may not be sufficient for verification.

actor_risk.description: User interaction relies heavily on the integrity of the frontend.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Malicious frontend can alter incentive addition details leading to misdirected tokens or incorrect rates.

assumptions: User interacts with a malicious Berachain Hub frontend.
The malicious frontend alters the vault address, token address, amount, or rate in the transaction request.
User signs the manipulated transaction without noticing.

mitigation.strategy: Wallet should provide clear details of the contract interaction and parameters; users should verify incentive details (vault, token, amount, rate).

description: A malicious Berachain Hub frontend tampers with the transaction details for adding incentives to a reward vault, causing the user to deposit tokens into the wrong vault, deposit the wrong token/amount, or set an incorrect rate.
